reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal claim piece-by-piece: S : Set (ℝ × ℝ) captures "subset of ℝ²", IsClosed S captures "closed", the hypothesis ∀ s ∈ S, s.1 ∈ Ioo a b precisely expresses "lies in a < x < b" (since Ioo a b = {x | a < x ∧ x < b}), and {y | ∃ x : ℝ, ⟨x,y⟩ ∈ S} is exactly the projection on the y-axis. The conclusion IsClosed on this set matches "its projection on the y-axis is closed". The explicit hypothesis a < b merely surfaces the implicit premise of the informal "a < x < b" strip; if a ≥ b were allowed, S would be forced empty and the conclusion would be trivial, so this does not change the mathematical content.
